question_text: Which of the following is an example of a contact force?

options.label: A
options.text: The gravitational force between the Earth and the Moon

options.label: B
options.text: The magnetic force between two bar magnets

options.label: C
options.text: The friction force between a sliding book and a table surface

options.label: D
options.text: The electrostatic force between a charged balloon and a wall

correct_answer: The friction force between a sliding book and a table surface
explanation: Contact forces only occur when two objects are touching each other. Friction between the book and table is a contact force because the surfaces are in direct physical contact. Gravitational, magnetic, and electrostatic forces are non-contact forces because they can act at a distance without the objects touching.
